title: Which Dermal Filler Is Best For Under Eyes？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Which Dermal Filler Is Best For Under Eyes？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So which filler is best for andyrise [Music] so what i would recommend is that you always choose products that have got data behind them where possible or that you've got for some reason a large amount of experience with already if you're completely new to it the best form of dat…

segments.text: so which filler is best for andyrise [Music] so what i would recommend is that you always choose products that have got data behind them where possible or that you've got for some reason a large amount of experience with already if you're completely new to it the best form of data that i'm aware of is fda approval so any product that is fda approved has an edge on other products because you've got real clinical data based on real patients having procedures which will tell you how the product behaves that doesn't mean it's actually any better than any other products but it knows it's a known entity you know what it does and you know that the manufacturer is prepared to back it which means it's not going to be on the far end of the bell curve of complications or anything because they've tested it so in this case for periorbital treatments i believe tso density 2 is now licensed in america by the fda for treatment and in the uk tm i use and have been using for many years juvederm volbella i'm extremely happy with it it's recommended by the

manufacturer i do not believe they yet have a license in the states for it and but i bet i use it on the basis of the recommendation of the manufacturer they're very reputable probably the biggest dermal filler company and then my experience over the last seven years which has been really favorable i think is a great product so that's how i would choose it if you're new to it does make sense to go with fda approved products and secondary would be manufacturer approval and i would rate friends who do treatments as quite low on the reason on that basis alone because you simply don't know if someone's been injecting for six months and they've learned tear trough the chance are they not they've not done that many procedures they don't know what the product does over two or three years you're looking for someone with a wide amount of experience with their product so that they've seen the patients who come back two or three years later because really to know a product you've got to have used it for years it doesn't help that you like injecting on the day unfortunately because that's only a very small part of the product characteristics so yeah those are different ways you might decide to look at it
but i hope that helps you
